summary: For 4 of 5 tickers (BOX, NTAP, WDAY, WMS) the convention flip IS detectable from prior-10-Q XBRL history (cal_year(period_end) - DocumentFiscalYearFocus != 0 across history -> 'year_of_start XBRL' signal, agent5 H2). For GIII the divergence is genuinely irreducible at the XBRL layer: GIII's XBRL always reports year_of_start (delta=0 cal_year-vs-XBRL_FY) while EX-99.1 always says year_of_end -- structurally identical to SAFE issuers like ACI from XBRL alone.

BOX.reasoning: BOX's XBRL convention FLIPPED at fiscal Q1 2025 (period 2024-04-30): prior to the flip, dei:DocumentFiscalYearFocus matched the year-of-end of the fiscal year (e.g., 10-K period 2023-01-31 -> FY=2023; 10-Q 2023-04-30 -> FY=2024). After the flip, FY stops incrementing at the 10-K -> first 10-Q transition (10-K period 2024-01-31 -> FY=2024; 10-Q period 2024-04-30 -> FY=2024). EX-99.1 stayed at year-of-end ('Fiscal Q1 2025'). The structural signature: prior 10-Q delta = cal_year(period_end) - DocumentFiscalYearFocus is mixed across history ({-1: 3, 0: 6} per agent5 H2). Detectable. CurrentFiscalYearEndDate is consistently --01-31 (calendar-month, not 52/53-week).

rows_checked.advance_one_quarter_from_prior_10Q: prior 10-K (2023-01-31) had FY=2023 FY -> q4_to_q1 advance gives FY=2023 Q1 (matches companion XBRL)

rows_checked.companion_xbrl_dei_quote: Companion 10-Q period_of_report=2023-07-31, accession=0001558370-23-015551, XBRL FY=2023 Q=Q2 per master_truth.csv
rows_checked.advance_one_quarter_from_prior_10Q: prior 10-Q (2023-04-30) had FY=2023 Q1 -> q1_to_q2 advance gives FY=2023 Q2

GIII.reasoning: GIII is the genuinely irreducible case. dei:DocumentFiscalYearFocus is ALWAYS aligned with cal_year of period_of_report (delta=0 across all 9 prior 10-Q rows per agent5 H2 -- 'all_zero_n=9'), so XBRL says 'year_of_start' consistently. EX-99.1 ALWAYS says year_of_end ('FIRST QUARTER FISCAL 2024' for fiscal year ending Jan 31, 2024). The disagreement lives ENTIRELY in EX-99.1 prose: GIII's XBRL DEI is structurally identical to SAFE issuer ACI's (both delta=0) while their EX-99.1 conventions diverge. No XBRL-visible signal separates GIII from ACI.

NTAP.reasoning: NTAP's XBRL flipped at fiscal Q1 ending July 2025 (period 2025-07-25 -> FY=2025 instead of expected FY=2026 under year_of_end). Earlier years used year_of_end (10-K period 2023-04-28 -> FY=2023; 10-Q 2023-07-28 -> FY=2024 -- incremented). Then 10-K period 2025-04-25 -> FY=2025; 10-Q period 2025-07-25 -> FY=2025 (NOT incremented). EX-99.1 stayed at year_of_end ('Second Quarter of Fiscal Year 2026'). agent5 H2 score: '10Q deltas mixed: {0: 5, -1: 4}' -- detectable as UNSAFE_PRED. NTAP is also a 52/53-week filer (FYE varies Apr 24-28), so '52/53-week' is NOT the discriminator -- the delta-vs-cal-year signal is.

WDAY.reasoning: WDAY's XBRL flipped at fiscal Q1 ending Apr 2024. Pre-flip: 10-K 2023-01-31 -> FY=2023; 10-Q 2023-04-30 -> FY=2024 (incremented). Post-flip: 10-K 2024-01-31 -> FY=2024; 10-Q 2024-04-30 -> FY=2025; but then on 10-K 2025-01-31 -> FY=2025; 10-Q 2025-04-30 -> FY=2025 (NOT incremented anymore). EX-99.1 stays at year_of_end ('Fiscal 2026 Q2/Q3'). agent5 H2 score: '10Q deltas mixed {-1: 7, 0: 4}' -> UNSAFE_PRED. CurrentFiscalYearEndDate=1/31 throughout (calendar-month). Detectable.

WMS.reasoning: WMS XBRL is most chaotic: it flips year_of_start <-> year_of_end multiple times AND uses wrong DocumentFiscalPeriodFocus quarter labels (e.g., reports Q3 for what's calendar-Q2 of the fiscal year). agent5 H2 score: '10Q deltas mixed {-1: 3, 0: 4}' -> UNSAFE_PRED. CurrentFiscalYearEndDate=--03-31 always (calendar-month, not 52/53-week). Detectable as UNSAFE by H2.

tested_signals: DEI:EntityCommonStockSharesOutstanding presence (not tested, no signal hypothesis)
fiscal_year_end_month (tested by agent5 H1 -- REJECTED: LOW/RH SAFE with fye=1; CNM/PVH/PLCE/DKS UNSAFE with fye=2)
52_53_week_marker via CurrentFiscalYearEndDate variability (tested by agent5 H3 -- REJECTED: NTAP UNSAFE 52/53wk; ROST SAFE month-end)
filing cadence pattern (tested by agent5 H5 -- UNTESTABLE without granular cadence data)

XBRL prior 10-Q delta = cal_year(period_of_report) - DocumentFiscalYearFocus across history (agent5 H2 -- PROMISING_PARTIAL: zero false positives across 18 SAFE tickers, catches BOX/NTAP/NTNX/WDAY/WMS, MISSES GIII)
Multi-prior stable offset using sec_truth_FY (agent5 H4 -- not PIT-allowed; demonstrates separation but unshippable)
EX-99.1 announcement convention vs XBRL DEI divergence (agent5 H6 -- REJECTED: information lives in EX-99.1 prose only, not in XBRL DEI surfaces)
Stale XBRL FY |delta|>=2 (agent5 H7 -- partial value, catches CNM only)
structural_discriminator_search.details: agent5's H2 (rule: trust XBRL advance only if for ALL prior 10-Qs of this ticker, cal_year(prior_period_of_report) == prior_xbrl_DocumentFiscalYearFocus across history; else fail-closed) is a CLEAN SAFE-side gate (18/18 SAFE tickers correctly classified, 0 false positives) and catches 4 of the 5 unsafe tickers in this audit (BOX, NTAP, WDAY, WMS) -- their XBRL filers either flipped convention or have inconsistent year_of_start/year_of_end across history, making the delta non-zero somewhere. GIII is the GENUINELY irreducible case: its XBRL is consistently year_of_start (delta=0 across all 9 prior 10-Q rows -- 'all_zero_n=9' per agent5), structurally identical to SAFE issuer ACI's XBRL (also all_zero_n=10). The convention divergence (XBRL year_of_start vs EX-99.1 year_of_end) is visible only in EX-99.1 prose; from XBRL DEI alone GIII and ACI are indistinguishable. Result: 4 of 5 unsafe tickers are NOT strictly irreducible (a deployable gate exists); GIII alone is genuinely irreducible without parsing EX-99.1 text or maintaining a per-ticker convention table.

caveats: BOX 8-K 2024-08-27 EX-99.1 quote: I directly extracted 'Second Quarter 2025' (with 'Fiscal' prefix elsewhere in the document) via grep. master_truth.csv records the full literal as 'Fiscal Second Quarter 2025'; my row text references the longer phrase via the master record.
BOX 8-K 2025-08-26 EX-99.1: 'Second Quarter Fiscal 2026 Financial Results' was extracted directly from raw_sec/0000950170-25-111447/box-ex99_1.htm with grep.

BOX 10-Q 2024-07-31: dei:DocumentFiscalYearFocus=2024 confirmed by direct grep of raw HTML (raw_sec/0000950170-24-102328/box-20240731.htm) producing 'font-family:Times New Roman;min-width:fit-content;">2024'.
BOX 10-Q 2025-07-31: dei:DocumentFiscalYearFocus=2025 confirmed by direct grep of raw HTML (raw_sec/0000950170-25-111701/box-20250731.htm).
GIII 10-Q 2023-04-30: dei:DocumentFiscalYearFocus=2023 and dei:DocumentFiscalPeriodFocus=Q1 confirmed by direct grep of raw HTML (raw_sec/0001558370-23-010832/giii-20230430x10q.htm).
NTAP 10-Q 2025-10-24: DocumentFiscalYearFocus=2025 confirmed by direct grep of raw HTML (raw_sec/0001193125-25-297237/ntap-20251024.htm).

NTAP 10-Q 2026-01-23 (Q3 row): companion XBRL FY/Q taken from periodic_calendar_profile.csv master record, not re-verified from raw HTML, because the row was non-FAIL_CLOSED in earlier columns and the structural pattern is established.

WMS 10-Q 2024-06-30 (FY=2024 Q1) and 2024-09-30 (FY=2025 Q2): both directly verified via grep of raw HTML showing dei:DocumentFiscalYearFocus values 2024 and 2025 respectively -- demonstrating the intra-fiscal-year XBRL convention flip.
WMS 10-Q 2023-09-30 has companion_xbrl_q='Q3' (issuer-mislabeled -- calendar shape is Q2 of fiscal year ending March 2024). This is independent of the year_of_start/year_of_end issue.

agent5_discriminator.json (already in subagent_findings/) had performed the comprehensive discriminator search -- I cross-checked its H1-H7 results and confirm them by spot-checking individual ticker XBRL DEI values. Where I rely on agent5's machine-aggregated counts (10Q delta histograms) rather than re-running the aggregation, I cite agent5_discriminator.json explicitly in the ticker reasoning.

Mechanism summary: 'XBRL is year_of_start, EX-99.1 is year_of_end' is the consistent post-flip pattern for BOX (FY2025+), NTAP (FY2026+), WDAY (FY2025+), and WMS (mixed). For GIII it holds throughout history. Within the BOX/NTAP/WDAY group, the flip is a CHANGE in XBRL filer behavior; within GIII the convention has been static.
